name: AWQ
oneliner: Identifies the most important ~1% of weight channels via activation magnitudes, then protects them with per-channel scaling.
explanation: Not all weights matter equally. AWQ measures importance by activation statistics: channels with consistently large activations amplify weight errors. Instead of mixed-precision (which kills hardware efficiency), AWQ multiplies important weight channels by scale s before quantization, divides corresponding activations by s. Mathematically equivalent but reduces relative quantization error for salient channels. Only needs forward-pass stats — fast (minutes for 70B), no gradients or Hessian.
fundamentals: 1) Calibrate: s_x(j) = mean(|X[:,j]|) per channel j. 2) Compute scaling: s(j) = s_x(j)^$\alpha$, $\alpha$ found by grid search minimizing $\|\|Q(W·diag(s))·(X/diag(s)) - WX\|\|$. 3) Store W' = W·diag(s), quantize W'. 4) At runtime: activations divided by s (fused into preceding LayerNorm).

foundational_papers.title: AWQ: Activation-aware Weight Quantization for LLM Compression and Acceleration
